title: Research Harness Configuration Manifest
description: THE deploy contract (SPEC §7): the one file a clone edits to declare its topics, research dimensions, output targets, and packs. Modeled on langgraph.json's role as the single declarative manifest (SPEC §4).

mifConformanceLevel.description: Minimum MIF conformance level the harness emits AND ingests. SPEC §10 fixes this at Level 3 (provenance + citations + entities + extensions). The floor binds every artifact crossing the project boundary — findings, generic markdown reports, and ingested source envelopes — not findings alone. Orthogonal-format channel outputs (blog, book, pdf, audio, external-service bodies) are the only exceptions, and only when explicitly declared exempt (outputs[].mifExempt here for first-class channels, or the pack manifest's `.claude-plugin/plugin.json` mif.exempt for channel packs).

features.description: Opt-in feature flags. The template ships strict; each flag defaults to false and an instance enables only what it needs. Off-by-default keeps the core's guarantees intact for clones that don't opt in.

internalCitations.description: When true, the citation-integrity gate also accepts internal/document citations (citationType ^internal: carrying the quoted evidence in `note`) as traceable, alongside http(s) URLs. For importing legacy corpora whose findings cite internal documents (ADRs, named reports) without a web URL. Default false (strict http-only).

voice.description: Voice enforcement for authored prose, applied by the check-voice.sh hook to the publication channels (canonical report, blog, book). Optional and generic: absent, the gate runs its baseline (AI-character punctuation + buzzword checks; contractions allowed). A consumer sets `profile` to their human-voice profile id and toggles `rules` to match it. Authoring/adding the actual prose profiles is the human-voice plugin's job (multiple profiles supported); this block only selects the active profile the gate names and the deterministic rules it enforces.

profile.description: The human-voice profile id the gate names and authors apply via /human-voice:human-voice (e.g. "default", or a consumer profile id). Default "default".

rules.description: Deterministic mechanical rules the gate enforces; each defaults to the generic published-prose baseline.

noAiPunctuation.description: Reject em/en dash, ellipsis glyph, bullet glyph, smart quotes, and emoji in authored prose. Default true.

noContractions.description: Reject contractions. A profile-specific style, off by default since most voices permit them. Default false.

warnBuzzwords.description: Warn (non-blocking) on AI buzzwords and filler phrases. Default true.

topics.description: The multi-topic registry. Each topic maps to one MIF namespace under reports/<topic>/.

namespace.description: MIF namespace path that owns this topic's memory units.

ontologies.description: Per-topic ontology binding (SPEC §8c). Each entry is an ontology id or id@version that MUST be enabled in the top-level `ontologies[]` control plane (the catalog). The vendored core (mif-base) is global to every topic; only ENABLED extended ontologies listed here apply to this topic's findings. Findings under this topic may only be ontology-typed against a bound ontology.

Ontology control plane (SPEC §8c): the enable toggle for the vendored ontology data packs under packs/ontologies/. Disabled (default) ontologies are vendored and conformance-validated but are NOT cataloged, so no topic may bind them. sync-packs.sh writes the enabled subset into the catalog (.claude/enabled-packs.json `ontologies[]`); topics[].ontologies may bind only cataloged ids.

dimensions.description: Config-declared, domain-general research dimensions the dimension-analyst fans out across (SPEC §4a). Not the fixed market-research set.

pack.description: Optional: the methodology pack that contributes this dimension (omit for core dimensions).

outputs.description: Enabled output channels. blog is the always-on first-class output (SPEC §6d); book and other channels arrive via optional channel packs. The generic `report` channel (reports/<topic>/<slug>.md) is the canonical MIF Level-3 source of truth and is never exempt; the channels here are projections of it.

mifExempt.description: When true, this first-class channel's rendered output is exempt from the MIF Level-3 output-conformance gate (SPEC §10) because its format is orthogonal to the result (e.g. blog/book public prose, which the citation-leak gate keeps free of internal MIF identity). The gate skips and logs exempt surfaces.

mifExemptReason.description: Why the format is orthogonal to the result (required to make an exemption auditable).

freshness.description: Source-type decay for finding freshness (SPEC §11): how long a verified finding stays fresh before it is re-falsified on the next reshape/update. A finding's window is the MIN over its citations' MIF citationType values (the most volatile cited source governs); re_verify_by = verification.attempted_at + window. Absent = the harness default (default_days for all).

default_days.description: Staleness window (days) for any citationType not listed in by_citation_type.

by_citation_type.description: Per-MIF-citationType staleness windows in days. Keys are MIF citationType values (article, book, paper, website, documentation, repository, video, podcast, specification, dataset, tool, other).

packs.description: The pack control plane (SPEC §7b): each entry enables/disables a pack and declares its source. Materialized into Claude Code's enabledPlugins at instantiation.

oneOf.description: An external or private plugin the clone is entitled to.
